title: When does fast music help a store? — Daniel Fox
meta_description: Fast music isn't worse or better than slow. The research shows it speeds turnover, can lift impulse buying, and can backfire into stress — depending on how crowded the store is and what you want customers to do.

schema.headline: When does fast music help a store (and when does it backfire)?
schema.description: What the research shows about fast-tempo music, turnover, impulse buying, and the point where arousal tips into stress.
